rows.notes: Adopted gemini-flash's description, row type, and amounts. Pounds value 3644 confirmed visually; the second digit is clearly a '6' in the script, not '0'. Row type 'total' is consistent with 'Brought up' and context.

Adopted gemini-flash for description and amounts after visual verification. The name is 'Osborne', and the item is 'small tiles', not 'Johnson - smoke bills'. Pounds is '1', not '4', and shillings is '4', not '7'.
rows.english_description: Small roof tiles purchased from Osborne for use at Kidlington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Small roof tiles purchased from Osborne for use at Kidlington, amounting to £1 4s 2d.

rows.description: Bates - Trees for Rectory at Kidlington & Planting
rows.notes: Amounts are consistent between both extractors. Adopted gemini-flash's description as more accurate for the name ('Bates' vs 'Pates'), item ('Trees' vs 'Fees'), and place ('Kidlington' vs 'Riddington').
rows.english_description: Trees purchased and planted for the rectory at Kidlington, paid to Bates.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Trees purchased and planted for the rectory at Kidlington, paid to Bates, amounting to £10 19s 3d.

rows.description: Parker - Books for Bishop of Montreal
rows.notes: Description and pence amount are consistent. Visually verified pounds as '5' (not '54') and shillings as '4' (not '7'). `gpt-5-mini` likely confused part of the description text with the pounds digit.
rows.english_description: Books purchased from Parker to be sent to the Bishop of Montreal.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Books purchased from Parker to be sent to the Bishop of Montreal, amounting to £5 4s 0d.

rows.description: Rose - late Kitchen Woman, Pay @ 5/ P week

rows.english_description_with_amount: Final payments to Rose, the late kitchen woman, paid at five shillings per week, amounting to £13 0s 0d.
